---
title: "Tutorial: Align text and audio"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: false
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
lightbox: true
reference-location: margin
freeze: auto
cache: true
conda:
    environment: easywhisperer
resources:
    ../_assets/tale_two_cities_ch01.txt
---

This tutorial will demonstrate the most straightforward case for aligning text and audio. It assumes we have access to a transcript that covers everything spoken in the audio.  

For this tutorial, we will be using:

* An audio recording of the first chapter of "A Tale of Two Cities", read by Paul Adams ([LibriVox](https://librivox.org/a-tale-of-two-cities-by-charles-dickens-2/)).
* The corresponding [text](https://www.gutenberg.org/cache/epub/98/pg98.txt) from [Project Gutenberg](https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/98).  

## Download text and audio

Let's begin by downloading the book and using a regular expression to extract the first chapter's text.

```{python}
import re
import requests

url = "https://www.gutenberg.org/cache/epub/98/pg98.txt"
full_text = requests.get(url).text

# Extract Chapter I text (i.e. the text between "CHAPTER I. The Period" and "CHAPTER II.")
match = re.search(
    r"(?<=CHAPTER I\.\r\nThe Period\r\n)[\s\S]+?(?=CHAPTER II\.)",
    full_text,
)
text = match.group().strip()
```

For reference, here's the text of the first chapter, before we move on to aligning it with the audio:

::: {.transcript-card .transcript-container}
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
print(text)
```
:::

Next, we download the audio and save it to disk.

```{python}
#| output: false
from huggingface_hub import snapshot_download

snapshot_download(
    "Lauler/easytranscriber_tutorials",
    repo_type="dataset",
    local_dir="data/tutorials",
    allow_patterns="tale-of-two-cities_short-en/*",
)
```

Here's the audio we'll be working with:

<div class="audio-card">
  <div class="audio-card-label">Sample audio</div>
  <div class="audio-card-title"><em>A Tale of Two Cities</em> — Chapter 1 (LibriVox)</div>
  <audio controls>
    <source src="https://huggingface.co/datasets/Lauler/easytranscriber_tutorials/resolve/main/tale-of-two-cities_short-en/taleoftwocities_01_dickens_64kb_trimmed.mp3"
    type="audio/mpeg">
  </audio>
</div>

## Align text and audio

The process for aligning the text and audio looks similar to the simple example in the [overview](overview.qmd) tutorial.
